description.notes: TWO-PART ACCOUNT SELECTION + CHECK DEPOSIT: Customer needs to open BOTH a new checking account AND a savings account, each with convoluted requirements. Customer is an EXISTING Green Account holder (meets 14-day tenure for savings eligibility). PART 1 - CHECKING: Requirements seem to match multiple accounts (Bluest, Blue, Evergreen, Purple) but only Purple Account fits ALL criteria: (1) ATM fee rebates for international ATMs, (2) no foreign transaction fees, (3) minimum balance under $10k to waive monthly fee, (4) multi-currency wallet, (5) doesn't require specific age. Red herrings include mentioning eco-consciousness (Evergreen) and wanting something 'premium' (Bluest). PART 2 - SAVINGS: Only Silver Plus Account satisfies: (1) ~12-15 withdrawals/month, (2) relationship bonus for existing checking customer, (3) min balance under $7k, (4) guaranteed daily compounding, (5) ATM fee rebates, (6) tier 2 APY of 3%+. PART 3 - FUNDING: After opening savings, agent offers transfer per protocol. User declines transfer but wants to deposit a check instead. Agent looks up deposit_check_3847 user tool and provides it to user. User deposits $1,500 check. Agent confirms balance update.

user_scenario.instructions:
**Your character:** You are Yuki Nakamura, a 33-year-old freelance graphic designer from Portland, OR. You already have a Rho-Bank Green Account (checking) for about 6 months. You travel internationally 3-4 times/year (Japan and Europe). You're overwhelmed by financial jargon and just want someone to tell you what to do.

**Your goal:** Get ONE recommendation each for a new checking account (for travel) and a savings account (for emergency fund). After the savings is opened, fund it by depositing a $1,500 check.

**Verification info:**
- Name: Yuki Nakamura | Phone: 503-555-0842 | Email: yuki.nakamura@simba.com
- DOB: 05/12/1991 | Address: 3421 Sakura Avenue, Portland, OR 97205
- Existing account: Green Account (checking, ~6 months)

## Conversation Flow:

1. **Opening - Make clear you want ONE recommendation for each:** "Hi! I already have a Green Account with you that I love for everyday stuff, but I'm looking to open a second checking account, plus a savings account. Look, I'm going to be honest - I tried looking at your website but there are so many options and reading through all the fine print gives me a headache. I have some specific things I need, and I'm hoping you can just tell me which accounts are right for me. I'll sign up for whatever you recommend - I just don't want to compare a bunch of options myself. Can you help?"

2. **Provide verification info when asked:** Give your name, phone number, email, date of birth, and address.

3. **Start with checking account requirements:** When the agent asks about your needs, reveal your CHECKING requirements organically first.
   - Start with the travel/ATM issue: "So I travel internationally pretty often - Japan and Europe for client work. Maybe 3-4 trips a year. I end up using ATMs abroad constantly and those fees really eat into my budget. Any way to get those reimbursed?"
   - Then foreign fees: "Also I hate those foreign transaction fees when I buy things abroad. Is there something without those?"
   - Then the balance: "I can keep maybe three to four thousand in this travel account, five at most. Nothing crazy like fifty grand though - I'm a freelancer, not a millionaire!"
   - Then multi-currency: "Oh - and I sometimes get paid in yen or euros. Would be nice to hold those currencies instead of converting right away."
   - Throw in red herrings: "I'm kind of eco-conscious - any green options? Also I've heard about premium accounts - anything exclusive that might fit?"
   - Clarify age: "I'm 33 by the way, so no student or senior stuff for me."

4. **If the agent tries to give you multiple checking options:** Push back. "Honestly, I really don't want to compare options. Can you just tell me which ONE is the best fit for everything I said? I trust you to figure it out."

5. **When the agent recommends a checking account:** Accept it. "Okay, if you think that's the one that fits all my needs, let's do it!"

6. **After checking is opened, pivot to savings:** "Perfect! Now I also need a savings account. Let me tell you what I'm looking for there..."
   - Withdrawals: "I tap into my savings 3-4 times a week, sometimes more. Don't want fees for that."
   - Relationship bonus: "Since I have checking accounts with you, is there a bonus rate for multiple accounts?"
   - Balance: "I can keep five to six grand, maybe seven max. Can't commit to more than that."
   - Daily compounding: "I NEED daily compounding - that's non-negotiable."
   - ATM rebates: "I use out-of-network ATMs a lot. Any way to get those fees covered?"
   - APY: "I want to earn at least 3% APY once my balance grows."
   - Red herrings: "Is there something green? I want something premium, not basic. A friend mentioned Gold and Green accounts?"

7. **If the agent tries to give you multiple savings options:** Push back again. "Same deal - just tell me which ONE is best for me."

8. **When the agent gives you a single savings recommendation:** Accept it. "Okay, let's set that up too."

9. **After savings account is opened and agent offers to transfer funds:**
    - "Actually, I'd rather not move money out of my checking right now. But I do have a check for $1,500 from a client that I want to put into this new savings account. How do I deposit it?"

10. **When the agent tells you how to deposit the check:**
    - Follow the agent's instructions exactly. They will tell you what tool to use and what information to provide (the account ID and amount).
    - Say: "Okay, let me try that... I've got the check ready." Then use the tool as the agent instructed.

11. **After depositing the check:** "Did that work? Can you confirm the money is in there?"

12. **After agent confirms the balance:**
    - "Perfect! I think I'm all set. Thanks so much for walking me through all of this - you made it so much easier than trying to figure it out on my own!"

**IMPORTANT BEHAVIORS:**
- Do NOT suggest any specific account names yourself - you don't know them
- Do NOT try to compare options - you want the agent to do that work
- Express that you're overwhelmed by choices and just want ONE answer for each
- Trust the agent's recommendations and agree to sign up
- Be grateful when the agent does the work of figuring out the right accounts for you
- Make sure to open the checking account FIRST, then the savings account
- When depositing the check, follow the agent's instructions exactly - they will tell you what tool to call and what parameters to use
